title: Micrandra glabra
taxonomy: Micrandra glabra belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. This family consists of around 282 genera and over 10,000 species, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Micrandra glabra is a species of plant within the genus Micrandra, which is a small genus of flowering plants. The classification of Micrandra glabra is further supported by its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies. By understanding the classification of Micrandra glabra, researchers can better comprehend its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
uses: Micrandra glabra is reported to have various medicinal uses, but its efficacy and safety as a treatment for any condition have not been extensively scientifically validated.
